Ventura County Coastal Association of Realtors (VCCAR), I would like to invite the Department to participate as a panelist at our 2026 Real Estate Summit on Thursday, May 14 at the Ventura Beach Marriott from 9:00 a.m. to 5:30 p.m.  One of our featured sessions will focus on wildfire prevention, home hardening strategies, and the evolving landscape of local and state policies related to wildfire resilience. As these issues continue to shape the future of housing and development in Ventura County, we are bringing together experts who can provide both practical guidance and policy insight.  The audience will include real estate professionals, community leaders, and policymakers who are deeply interested in understanding how wildfire-related challenges and regulations are impacting the housing market.

BC- Westminster Small Business Summit  Thank you for participating as an exhibitor at the Westminster Small Business Summit, hosted by the City of Westminster in partnership with the Orange County Inland Empire Small Business Development Center.  We are pleased to have your organization join us and appreciate your support in making this event a valuable resource for Westminster’s business community.  Exhibitor Arrival & Setup Information  Exhibitor Check-In: Begins at 7:00 a.m. - and all exhibitors must be checked in by 7:45 a.m.  Location: Westminster Rose Center, 14140 All American Way, Westminster CA 92683  Loading/Unloading: Please use the curb in front of the Westminster Rose Center  Event Parking: Parking Structure directly across from the venue.  Parking Fee: Parking for this event is free from 7:00 a.m. - 4:00 p.m.  Exhibitor Entry for Set Up: Enter through the patio, under the awning. If you have heavy or bulky items, you may enter the front lobby but must check-in with City staff first under the awning.  Table Assignment: Your organization’s name will be taped to your assigned table  Table: A 6-foot table will be provided, but the City is not providing tablecloths.  Provided by City: One 6-foot table per confirmed exhibitor  Important Exhibitor Requirements  Exhibitor Check in no earlier than 7a.m., and no later than 7::45 a.m.  All exhibitors must remain through the conclusion of the program at 2:00 p.m.  Load-out will begin promptly at 2:00 p.m.  All exhibitors must confirm your participation by replying to this email and please include the names (First and last), email address, or phone number of each attendee.  Event Schedule  8:00 a.m. – Resource Fair and Registration  9:00 a.m. – Welcome  10:00 a.m. – Breakout Session 1  Track 1: Funding, Financial Strategy, Performance, and Sales Growth  Funding Beyond the Basics: Choosing the Right Capital at the Right Time  Track 2: Cash Flow Management and Operational Efficiency  From Data to Dollars: Turning Digital Insights into Real Sales  11:00 a.m. – Breakout Session 2  Track 1: Funding, Financial Strategy, Performance, and Sales Growth  Cash Flow First: Managing Rising Costs Without Killing Growth  Track 2: Cash Flow Management and Operational Efficiency  Smart Systems & AI Tools: Streamlining Operations Without Breaking the Bank  12:00 p.m. – Lunch & Program: Doing Business in Westminster  During the program, exhibitors will be given an opportunity to speak for 1-3 minutes regarding their services they offer.  If you have any questions on the day of the event, please call my cell directly at (562) 305-3535. Thank you again for your participation. We look forward to a successful event with you.

Assembly District 52: Zone Zero Workshop Coordination Call  Good afternoon, Brenda,  Thanks for taking the time to attend Tuesday’s coordination call.  As we previously discussed, we think it would be valuable to show a few visuals while you discuss the intersection of Zone Zero, wildfire safety, and insurance. I look forward to you sending a few slides (from existing presentations or otherwise). If possible, please share these visuals with me by 9:00 AM, tomorrow, May 8. If there are any other resources that you regularly share with property owners and would be valuable to have on site, please let me know. We are still working to compile a list of resources to distribute to all our attendees.  For our prep documents, please share the phonetic spelling/pronunciation of Deputy Commissioner Juarez’s name. Once our run of show is finalized, I plan to share that with you as well.  We see your role as informing folks about what efforts the Department of Insurance has taken to help people keep or renew their insurance policies. Promoting clarity on your role, combatting misinformation, and limiting confusion is what we are looking for. What we discussed on the call around education and existing programs sounded good, as well as some of the more specific flags that came up. I’ll let you tell me what the best approach to achieve our goal is.  For your awareness, we anticipate there may be some attendees from the Altadena area.  Reach out at any time with other questions. We will be in touch soon! I will share additional information about Q&A in the coming days.  Best,  Nick Thell  Field Representative  Assemblymember Jessica M. Caloza  52 nd Assembly District  Website | Instagram | Facebook  State Capitol Office: (916) 319-2052  Los Angeles Office: (213) 483-5252  Sign up to receive our newsletter!

Subject: RE: Public Comment: Request for Council Support – Wildfire Recovery Legislation  From: Flores, Dystanie  Sent: Tuesday, April 28, 2026 11:59 AM  To: 'city_clerk@cityofglendora.gov' Subject: Public Comment: Request for Council Support – Wildfire Recovery Legislation  Mayor and Councilmembers,  My name is Dystanie Flores, and I serve as an Outreach Analyst with the California Department of Insurance.  I’m respectfully request your support for a package of wildfire recovery and insurance reform bills currently under consideration at the State Capitol. These measures are authored by Assemblymember’s Mike Gipson, Lisa Calderon and Senator Steve Padilla, and they are focused on addressing ongoing recovery challenges in wildfire-impacted communities across California.  The first bill, AB 1795, authored by Assemblymember Mike Gipson, would establish clear public health standards for smoke-damaged homes. After a wildfire, many homes may appear structurally sound but still contain hazardous smoke contamination. Without clear standards, homeowners often face delays, disputes, and uncertainty regarding remediation and insurance coverage. This bill creates clarity, protects public health, and helps streamline recovery.  The second bill, SB 876, authored by Senator Steve Padilla, strengthens disaster recovery protections and expands living expense coverage. Families displaced by wildfire are often out of their homes for extended periods. This measure helps ensure residents have adequate coverage for temporary housing and related costs, reducing financial hardship during a time of crisis.  The third bill, AB 1680, authored by Assemblymember Lisa Calderon, reforms and increases accountability within the FAIR Plan. As more Californians turn to the FAIR Plan due to insurance market instability, it is critical that the program operates with transparency, sustainability, and strong consumer protections. This bill aims to improve oversight and help stabilize the insurance market for wildfire-impacted communities.  Together, these measures are designed to speed recovery, reduce coverage gaps, increase accountability, and strengthen long-term community resilience.  Local government support is incredibly important in advancing policies that directly impact residents. I respectfully ask for your consideration and support.  I’m happy to provide fact sheets on each of these bills, along with a brief survey, and I would also welcome the opportunity to deliver a short 10-minute presentation at a future council meeting to provide a legislative update and answer any questions.  Thank you for your time, your leadership, and your continued commitment to protecting your community.  Dystanie Flores  Outreach Analyst  Community Relations and Outreach Branch  California Department of Insurance  300 South Spring Street, South Tower  Los Angeles, CA 90013  Dystanie.Flores@insurance.ca.gov Cell: (213) 220-7748  Twitter @CDINews Facebook @insurancecagov  [cid:image001.jpg@01DCD706.72C570F0]

Subject: Child Wellness & Safety Fair – CLCA Materials  Hello Colin,  I hope you’ve been well! I see your office is hosting an upcoming Child Wellness & Safety Fair.  I wanted to reach out and see if it would be okay for me to drop off materials for the California Low Cost Auto Insurance Program (CLCA) for your office to share with attendees.  CLCA is a state-sponsored program that helps income-eligible drivers get affordable, basic auto insurance so they can stay legally insured. It’s designed for good drivers who may be struggling with the cost of coverage, and it helps protect them and others on the road. To ensure we have a productive session, I wanted to provide a brief overview of the project we will be discussing: the Eaton Fire Mutual Insurance Collective.  The core objective is to discuss the possibilities of establishing a community-owned insurance entity specifically designed for fire survivors. The premise is that by incentivizing and requiring fire-resilient rebuilding standards (such as Class A roofing, ember-resistant vents, and non-combustible siding), we can effectively lower risk and, consequently, lower premiums for the community.  We are exploring a mutual insurance company structure in which policyholders act as owners, with any surplus reinvested in the community or used to reduce future premiums.  This Friday is a discovery meeting, and the primary goal is to bounce ideas, explore the feasibility of this model, and get your expert perspective on how we might navigate the regulatory and structural requirements to support these fire-resilience efforts.  I look forward to our conversation.  Best regards,  Carl D. Subject: Public Comment Submission - Support for Wildfire Recovery & Insurance  Reform Legislation  Mayor and Councilmembers,  My name is Judith Piña, and I serve as an Outreach Analyst with the California Department of Insurance and your local liaison to the Department.  I am writing to respectfully ask for your support for a package of wildfire recovery and insurance reform bills currently moving through the State Legislature. These measures are focused on addressing ongoing recovery challenges in wildfire-impacted communities across California.  These bills are sponsored by Insurance Commissioner Ricardo Lara and reflect feedback from wildfire survivors, as well as findings from ongoing claims investigations-highlighting the real challenges communities are facing during recovery.  The first bill, AB 1795 (Gipson), establishes clear public health standards for smoke-damaged homes. After a wildfire, homes may look intact but still contain hazardous smoke contamination. Without clear standards, homeowners often face delays, disputes, and uncertainty around cleanup and insurance coverage. This bill helps create clarity, protect public health, and support a smoother recovery process.  The second bill, SB 876 (Padilla), strengthens disaster recovery protections and expands living expense coverage. Families displaced by wildfire are often out of their homes for extended periods. This measure helps ensure residents have adequate coverage for temporary housing and related costs, reducing financial hardship during a time of crisis.  The third bill, AB 1680 (Calderon), focuses on improving accountability and transparency within the FAIR Plan. As more Californians turn to the FAIR Plan, it is critical that the program operates with transparency, sustainability, and strong consumer protection. This bill aims to strengthen oversight and support long-term market stability.  Together, these measures are designed to speed up recovery, reduce coverage gaps, increase accountability, and strengthen protection for consumers.  I would welcome the opportunity to provide a short 10-minute presentation at a future council meeting, and I will follow up with an email that includes a survey and fact sheets for your review.  Thank you for your time, Please feel free to reach out if you have questions  Warm regards,  Judith Pina  Outreach Analyst  Community Relations and Outreach Branch  California Department of Insurance  300 South Spring Street, South Tower  Los Angeles, CA 90013  (213) 507-9973  Judith.Pina@insurance.ca.gov Twitter @CDINews Facebook @insurancecagov  CONFIDENTIALITY NOTICE: This communication may contain confidential and/or legally privileged information. Subject: Follow-Up: Wildfire Recovery & Insurance Reform Bills – Materials and Survey  Dear Mayor and Councilmembers,  Good morning, I want to thank you again for the opportunity to email you for public comment at last night’s city council meeting regarding the pending wildfire recovery and insurance reform legislation currently under consideration at the State Capitol.  As a follow-up, I have attached the following materials for your review:  • Fact Sheet – AB 1795 (Smoke-Damaged Home Standards)  • Fact Sheet – SB 876 (Additional Living Expense Protections)  • Fact Sheet – AB 1680 (FAIR Plan Oversight and Accountability)  These measures are designed to support wildfire-impacted communities by establishing clear public health standards, strengthening financial protections for displaced families, and promoting greater transparency and sustainability within the FAIR Plan.  To help us better understand your position and answer any additional questions you may have, we kindly ask that you complete the brief support survey linked below:  https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://www.surveymonkey.com/r/BMWZX8Y__;!!NUwy8xNK!6DosTasXaOr4kEgJAXDJP3fjlH7s6phEMe4WRZqCfbTBigzgzZfyfOfWQQ06RaNLIqeKccQgGoVCi7DskHpXCRKzJp_HMbOtT1XR$  Your feedback is important, and we are happy to provide any additional information, meet individually, or present further details at your convenience.  Thank you again for your time, consideration, and continued leadership in supporting wildfire recovery efforts in our communities.  Dystanie Flores  Outreach Analyst  Community Relations and Outreach Branch  California Department of Insurance  300 South Spring Street, South Tower  Los Angeles, CA 90013  Dystanie.Flores@insurance.ca.gov Cell: (213) 220-7748  Twitter @CDINews Facebook @insurancecagov  [cid:image001.jpg@01DCAC86.69EA2520]  CONFIDENTIALITY NOTICE: This communication may contain confidential and/or legally privileged information. Subject: Thank You and Follow-Up on Insurance Options  Hi William,  I hope you’re doing well. I wanted to apologize for the delay in my response, and also sincerely thank you for the opportunity to present at your mobile home park. I truly appreciated the time you took to give me a tour; it was very helpful and a pleasure meeting with you.  I’m following up with information regarding insurance options for mobile homes that may be able to provide coverage outside of the FAIR Plan. I’ve included one insurance company in this email that you can reach out to for potential coverage options.  Additionally, I’ve provided a link below to our website’s Home Insurance Finder tool. This resource allows you to search by insurance type, distance, and ZIP code, and also filter for carriers that may write policies in high fire-risk areas like yours. Currently, the primary options for such areas are the FAIR Plan and California Casualty, both of which are noted for your reference.  I will also be mailing you informational resources. When you have a moment, could you please confirm the best mailing address for me to send that to?  Thank you again for your time and hospitality. Greetings Ms. Brenda Caloca:  The Speak Up Empowerment Foundation, Inc. and Partners are extremely excited about its upcoming signature event -the 10th Annual Ms. Single Mom Empowerment Forum! The program is being hosted on Saturday April 18, 2026, from 10am to 3pm.  This pre-Mother's Day program is our innovative gift to the community's female single parents - so, please take a quick look at why our program has been relevant to the community for the past ten years: MSMEF RECAP & Invit e.  We are in the process of confirming the subject matter experts for our panels - and would like to invite you to participate as our Business Insurance Requirements Resource this year.  I have attached the Panelist Form for your review & completion - and our Sponsor Deck is also attached for you to share.  * The attached form contains all of the speaker details on it.  * And we need your current headshot & short bio asap please!  We will host the program in person - and have proper Covid-19 protocols in place.  * The location is the beautiful Long Beach Marriott Grand Ballroom located at 4700 Airport Plaza Drive | Long Beach, CA 90815.  * The hours are from 10:00am to 3:00pm.  * The program includes motivating panel speakers, prizes, 3-course lunch, complimentary childcare, swag bags, and more. Meeting regarding an event and issues pertaining to AB38 Zone 0  Good morning, Brenda and Andrea!  I am so excited to have found your whole "new" level of information to share with my community!! As part of my "education efforts" please see the link below for a Vendors copy of our April community newsletter (with most names/phone #s removed). Page 31+ begins the Fire and Safety information. I don't think my April report conflicts with anything we talked about. Let me know if you cannot access the link.  https://drive.google.com/file/d/1xOnXS3lMQA_lqnMJDOxBV7_K7cQWZApt/view?usp=sharing  I am working on the May newsletter and will be incorporating your insurance information for my community update. I've also added you both to the Vendor's list so you can receive a link the last week in April.  Please let me know as soon as the meeting date in June is confirmed so I can reserve the facility - before someone else gets it! I think we discussed Tues as a good weekday option.  And, if possible for the meeting in June, could you please bring some printed copies of the handouts for display in the Fire Safety table in the Hall?  My sincere thanks again for all of your assistance,  Lynne  8975 Lawrence Welk Dr Spc 78  Escondido, CA 92026  (615) 491-1831  lynne.caples@gmail.com  On Mon, Mar 30, 2026 at 6:27?PM Caloca, Brenda < Brenda.Caloca@insurance.ca.gov > wrote:  Good evening, Ms. Caples,  It was such a great pleasure speaking with you earlier today. I am copying Andrea Valdes, an amazing colleague who represents your area. As promised, I am attaching the informational flyers, and we will follow up to confirm a date—either the first or second week of June at 4:00 p.m.—for an in?person presentation.  Attached, please find:  * Legislative Bills (One?Pager)  * Sustainable Insurance Strategy  * Building Communities Safer From Wildfire  * Statewide Learning Series  I am also including the wildfire recovery and insurance reform bills currently under consideration at the State Capitol. These measures, authored by Assemblymembers Mike Gipson, Lisa Calderon, and Senator Steve Padilla, focus on addressing ongoing recovery challenges in wildfire?impacted communities across California.  AB 1795 (Asm. Mike Gipson)  Establishes clear public health standards for smoke?damaged homes. After a wildfire, homes may appear structurally sound but still contain hazardous smoke contamination. Without defined standards, homeowners often face delays and disputes regarding remediation and insurance coverage. This bill provides clarity, protects public health, and streamlines recovery.  SB 876 (Sen. Steve Padilla)  Strengthens disaster recovery protections and expands living?expense coverage. Families displaced by wildfire are frequently out of their homes for extended periods. This bill helps ensure residents have adequate temporary housing coverage and reduces financial hardship during recovery.  AB 1680 (Asm. Lisa Calderon)  Improves accountability within the FAIR Plan. As more Californians turn to the FAIR Plan due to insurance market instability, it is critical that the program operates transparently, sustainably, and with strong consumer protections. This bill enhances oversight and helps stabilize the insurance market for wildfire?impacted areas.  Together, these measures aim to accelerate recovery, reduce coverage gaps, increase accountability, and strengthen long?term community resilience. Local government support is incredibly important in advancing policies that directly impact residents. We respectfully ask for your consideration and support. Fact sheets and sample letters of support are included.  Please let us know if you have any questions or if there is additional information we can provide. We look forward to meeting with you in June.  Warm Regards,  Brenda Caloca  I reached out to Lynne and had a great conversation with her. I’m including Andrea on this email to keep her in the loop since this opportunity is in her area.  Lynne was very appreciative of the information I shared, and she is interested in having us come out to present in June—either the first or second week—on a Tuesday at 4:00 pm. The audience will be adults 55+ in a manufactured home community, and there are approximately 457 units in the Champagne Village (Deer Springs) community. Ladies, please let me know how you’d like to proceed.  I’m sending her our Statewide Webinar information, the Safer from Wildfires flyer, and the letters of support for the sponsor bills.  Sharon, if there is anything else we should know about this group or the area, please let us know. Lastly, if you have and can share Chief Thomas Shoots’ contact information, that would be great. Warm Regards,  Tonya R. On March 23, I'd also filed a "complaint" online, with Case # 8710483, but you may be able to provide a better response. Here's from my online submission:  I am the Firewise leader for our community, and recently a homeowner with CalFAIR insurance contacted me with concern that CalFAIR expected the Zone 0, 5-foot, compliance for their CalFAIR insurance to continue. Our manufactured home community is also governed by Title 25, and Section 2, page 58 states we can have a minimum of 3 foot setbacks from our property line. CalFAIR photos and instructions are requiring her to trim/remove her neighbors bushes - this is wrong!  ALSO, the CalFAIR "requirements" conflict with the Firewise community "goals" that were projected over 3 years. As the Firewise leader, this is conflicting information - CalFAIR expects 100% compliance for the insurance, for a FireWise goal to achieve over 3 years? Please have consistent information.  For expectations: CalFAIR can expect Zone 0 to extend to the property line, which may only be 3 feet. And consistency in expectations for Firewise communities - are they "requirements" for the insurance or "goals" as FireWise marketed the program?  I've attached two documents that are referenced and have more from CalFAIR documentation (images and other letters) if needed. Please advise on how we can adjust expectations on the two issues:  * CalFAIR 5 foot Zone 0 vs. Title 25 3 foot setbacks. Homeowners should not be expected to clear their neighbors' bushes.  * Defensible Space goals, vs. requirements for insurance  Thank you for your time! Subject: FW: Public Comment: Request for Council Support – Wildfire Recovery Legislation  Mayor and Councilmembers.  My name is Dystanie Flores, and I serve as an Outreach Analyst with the California Department of Insurance.  I’m respectfully request your support for a package of wildfire recovery and insurance reform bills currently under consideration at the State Capitol. These measures are authored by Assemblymember’s Mike Gipson, Lisa Calderon and Senator Steve Padilla, and they are focused on addressing ongoing recovery challenges in wildfire-impacted communities across California.  The first bill, AB 1795, authored by Assemblymember Mike Gipson, would establish clear public health standards for smoke-damaged homes. After a wildfire, many homes may appear structurally sound but still contain hazardous smoke contamination. Without clear standards, homeowners often face delays, disputes, and uncertainty regarding remediation and insurance coverage. This bill creates clarity, protects public health, and helps streamline recovery.  The second bill, SB 876, authored by Senator Steve Padilla, strengthens disaster recovery protections and expands living expense coverage. Families displaced by wildfire are often out of their homes for extended periods. This measure helps ensure residents have adequate coverage for temporary housing and related costs, reducing financial hardship during a time of crisis.  The third bill, AB 1680, authored by Assemblymember Lisa Calderon, reforms and increases accountability within the FAIR Plan. As more Californians turn to the FAIR Plan due to insurance market instability, it is critical that the program operates with transparency, sustainability, and strong consumer protections. This bill aims to improve oversight and help stabilize the insurance market for wildfire-impacted communities.  Together, these measures are designed to speed recovery, reduce coverage gaps, increase accountability, and strengthen long-term community resilience.  Local government support is incredibly important in advancing policies that directly impact residents. I respectfully ask for your consideration and support.  I’m happy to provide fact sheets on each of these bills, along with a brief survey, and I would also welcome the opportunity to deliver a short 10-minute presentation at a future council meeting to provide a legislative update and answer any questions.  Thank you for your time, your leadership, and your continued commitment to protecting your community. My name is Dystanie Flores, and I serve as an Outreach Analyst with the California Department of Insurance. Thank you for the opportunity to speak with you today. I’m respectfully request your support for a package of wildfire recovery and insurance reform bills currently under consideration at the State Capitol. These measures are authored by Assemblymember’s Mike Gipson, Lisa Calderon and Senator Steve Padilla, and they are focused on addressing ongoing recovery challenges in wildfire-impacted communities across California. The first bill, AB 1795, authored by Assemblymember Mike Gipson, would establish clear public health standards for smoke-damaged homes. After a wildfire, many homes may appear structurally sound but still contain hazardous smoke contamination. Without clear standards, homeowners often face delays, disputes, and uncertainty regarding remediation and insurance coverage. This bill creates clarity, protects public health, and helps streamline recovery. The second bill, SB 876, authored by Senator Steve Padilla, strengthens disaster recovery protections and expands living expense coverage. Families displaced by wildfire are often out of their homes for extended periods. This measure helps ensure residents have adequate coverage for temporary housing and related costs, reducing financial hardship during a time of crisis. The third bill, AB 1680, authored by Assemblymember Lisa Calderon, reforms and increases accountability within the FAIR Plan. As more Californians turn to the FAIR Plan due to insurance market instability, it is critical that the program operates with transparency, sustainability, and strong consumer protections. This bill aims to improve oversight and help stabilize the insurance market for wildfire-impacted communities. Together, these measures are designed to speed recovery, reduce coverage gaps, increase accountability, and strengthen long-term community resilience. Local government support is incredibly important in advancing policies that directly impact residents. I respectfully ask for your consideration and support. I’m happy to provide fact sheets on each of these bills, along with a brief survey, and I would also welcome the opportunity to deliver a short 10-minute presentation at a future council meeting to provide a legislative update and answer any questions. Thank you for your time, your leadership, and your continued commitment to protecting your community. Text: Subject: RE: Invitation to Join Us at CARE FEST 2026  Good afternoon Oscar,  Thank you for the invitation to this much-needed event. I dropped off our California Low Cost Auto Insurance Program (CLCA) postcards at your office last week so you can share them at your table for tomorrow's event.  The CLCA is a state-sponsored program, created by the legislature to offer liability insurance to income-eligible (low-income) good and new drivers in CA. This program offers liability insurance for a year if a driver qualifies - regardless of their immigration status. The goal of the program is to help uninsured motorists comply with state law by having liability insurance.  How to qualify for this program?  * You must have a valid CA driver's license  * OR an AB 60 license  * The Value of the vehicle must be $25,000 or less  * Be at least 16 years old  * Meet income guidelines  * Cannot have a "bad" driving record  So it's available for good or new drivers that meet the income guidelines - regardless of their immigration status.  To apply, a driver will need:  * The proper license  * Valid Car Registration  * Down payment - 20% of annual premium  * And Proof of Income (it can be a paycheck stub, W2, or even a statement of benefit/card from CalFresh, Medi-CAL, or other state/county programs)  Drivers can apply online at https://urldefense.com/v3/__http://www.mylowcostauto.com__;!!NUwy8xNK!7Atz26RvDj2ob86kbE-1Q5lNz2cDAfKhXMFTKjgy-O7XUFtXW4WbEPj0vjZlulR29Efr-JNkKmyDt__Q3IoUMUygaJoH1dbY$ and take the eligibility questionnaire.  Drivers can apply directly through a certified agent and ask questions on the website via chat or by calling 866-602-8861.  Feel free to reach out to me if you need our postcards for your center or would like to share this resource in your newsletter or on social media.  Thanks, and wishing you a great event tomorrow.  Best regards,  Durriya Syed  Outreach Analyst  Community Relations and Outreach Branch  California Department of Insurance  (916) 300-1225 (Mobile)  From: Mendoza Plascencia, Oscar Sent: Monday, March 2, 2026 11:39 AM  To: CRC-Hawk Cares Subject: RE: Invitation to Join Us at CARE FEST 2026  Dear Community Partners,  I hope this message finds you well.  I am writing to inform you that we have made the decision to postpone CARE Fest to the upcoming semester so that we can intentionally align the event with our 10 year celebration of food access at Cosumnes River College. This milestone provides a meaningful opportunity to reflect on the growth of our efforts, honor the partnerships that have sustained this work, and recommit to advancing basic needs support for our students.  Over the past decade, Cosumnes River College has continued to expand its impact strengthening food access, housing support, and essential resources to ensure our students can persist and thrive. Aligning CARE Fest with this anniversary will allow us to elevate the celebration and more fully recognize the collective commitment that has made this work possible.  Thank you for your continued leadership and support of our students and the mission of the Hawk CARES Center. We are deeply appreciative of your partnership and hope you will be able to join us next semester as we celebrate 10 years of advancing food access and basic needs support at Cosumnes River College.  With gratitude,  Hawk CARES Center  Web https://urldefense.com/v3/__http://www.crc.losrios.edu__;!!NUwy8xNK!7Atz26RvDj2ob86kbE-1Q5lNz2cDAfKhXMFTKjgy-O7XUFtXW4WbEPj0vjZlulR29Efr-JNkKmyDt__Q3IoUMUygaJkIWr4O$ Email CRC-HawkCARES@crc.losrios.edu Phone #: (916) 691-7095  8401 Center Parkway Sacramento, CA 95823  [cid:image001.png@01DCBADF.580072D0]

Hi Judith,  We connected a few years back, but it has been a while. Paulette and Elba have been critical to our effort to educate folks on being more responsible for understanding their homeowner's policies.  We would love to talk about current changes, challenges and community perceptions.  Would you have time in the near future to talk? via Zoom or in-person? And It would be great to have you at an upcoming IEFSA meeting (as you did once before). IEFSA is expanding into the Riverside County area and would really like to have you be part of that, if it is in your territory.  Thank you  Laura  Laura Dyberg  President  MRFSC/IEFSA  909-528-6975  On Mon, Mar 16, 2026 at 4:56?PM Paulette Bunyapanasarn > wrote:  Hello Judith /California Department of Insurance,  It was a pleasure meeting you at the Fire Safe event in Temecula on Saturday, March 14, 2026.  I wanted to briefly introduce you to Ms. Laura Dyberg with the Fire Safe Council for Southern California, as we believe there may be value in connecting given your respective work in this space, particularly regarding property insurance and the increasing community need for information for 2026.  Our agency operates as a hybrid insurance brokerage under the flagship of Farmers Insurance while also functioning as an independent broker. This allows us to offer coverage options to the public through other insurance carriers outside of Farmers, including admitted and non-admitted markets as well as the FAIR Plan.  Through our volunteer work with the Fire Safe Councils, we help support community insurance workshops. These efforts are conducted strictly as educational outreach and are non-solicitation in nature. We do not represent any political organizations or specific insurance companies in these sessions. Our role as licensed insurance professionals is simply to help educate property owners so they can better understand their policies, communicate risk effectively using proper insurance terminology, and learn about potential coverage considerations, cost factors, and risk-reduction opportunities. The goal is to help residents become better informed so they can review their insurance coverage with their own insurance broker.  I’ll let the two of you take it from here if it makes sense to explore potential alignment or simply exchange perspectives for future outreach efforts, including a possible guest speaking at our insurance workshop planned for April 21, depending on your availability.  Thank you both for your time, and I look forward to you connecting. Subject: Need support for bills and info regarding wildfire insurance issues  Hi Giselle,  I really enjoyed our conversation today! It is always good to catch up with you! Thank you very much for everything you do for your community and those around you. I so appreciate your warmth and invitation to share the info at your meeting today. I've attached the fact sheet on the three bills for you; you're welcome to share it with your contacts.  As I shared with you, the Insurance Commissioner Ricardo Lara is sponsoring three important bills designed to strengthen consumer protections, improve claims handling, and ensure wildfire survivors and high?risk areas receive the support they need: AB 1680 (Make It FAIR Act), SB 876 (Disaster Recovery Reform Act), and AB 1795 (Smoke Damage Recovery Act).  As leaders in wildfire mitigation and community resilience, your voice is essential. We are asking Fire Safe Councils across the state to contact their State Senators and Assemblymembers and urge them to support these measures.  How these bills support fire?prone communities:  AB 1680 – Make It FAIR Act (Fact Sheet) * Strengthens FAIR Plan claims handling  * Expands and modernizes coverage options  * Increases transparency and oversight  * Ensures the FAIR Plan operates as a reliable safety net  SB 876 – Disaster Recovery Reform Act (Fact Sheet) * Improves claims timelines and communication for disaster survivors  * Strengthens consumer protections during recovery  * Ensures insurers follow clear standards after catastrophic events  * Reduces delays that leave families in limbo  AB 1795 – Smoke Damage Recovery Act (Fact Sheet) * Health-driven standards for smoke damaged homes  * Uniform insurance claims handling and restoration protocols  * Health-based guidelines to determine when a home is safe for families to return  How You Can Take Action  When lawmakers hear directly from community wildfire leaders, it highlights the urgent need for stronger, more responsive insurance protections.  Below are links to sample letters of support. Please feel free to personalize and send them to your legislative representatives:  * Letter of Support AB 1680 (Make it FAIR Act) * Letter of Support SB 876 (Disaster Recovery Reform Act) * Letter of Support AB 1795 (Smoke Damage Recovery Act) Additionally, below is the list of insurers who have submitted SIS?compliant filings and are approved:  Insurance Companies Approved:  1. Mercury – Approved; effective date 7/1/2026  2. CSAA – Approved; effective date 3/1/2026  3. USAA (four different USAA insurance companies) – Approved; effective date 4/30/2026  4. Pacific Specialty – Approved; effective date 7/1/2026  5. Text: (I gave the following to Zuleimy to follow up on March 10, 2026)  My name is Debbie Bernard and I'm the Firewise coordinator for The Woodlands HOA located in Simi Valley. Your name was given to me by Natasha Saxena, Regional Firewise Coordinator for the Ventura Regional Fire Safe Council. I'm looking for someone who can provide our homeowners with an update on what's taking place in the homeowners insurance market for those that live in high-fire areas, like our HOA. To date, homeowners receive large annual premium increases and/or insurance cancellation notices which pushes homeowners into the CA Fair Plan. Natasha mentioned that you may not be the right contact, but that hopefully you could point me in the right direction. I'm envisioning something simple like a zoom call our members could join, or if there is someone local to Simi, we could set up something in-person...may a 15-30 minute session talking about the current home insurance market/high fire-risk areas. Or maybe you already have a standard presentation since I'm sure we aren't the only ones wondering what's taking place and what we can expect to see in the future regarding insurance premiums.  Any assistance you can provide is most appreciated.  Regards,  Debbie Bernard  The Woodlands HOA  Firewise Coordinator  Ph/text: 805-433-4915  email: debbiebernard@yahoo.com

Text: Subject: FW: Public E- Comment: Request for Council Support – Wildfire Recovery Legislation  From: Flores, Dystanie  Sent: Tuesday, March 10, 2026 10:53 AM  To: 'city_clerk@cityofglendora.gov' Subject: Public E- Comment: Request for Council Support – Wildfire Recovery Legislation  Mayor and Councilmembers.  My name is Dystanie Flores, and I serve as an Outreach Analyst with the California Department of Insurance. Thank you for the opportunity to speak with you today.  I’m respectfully request your support for a package of wildfire recovery and insurance reform bills currently under consideration at the State Capitol. These measures are authored by Assemblymember’s Mike Gipson, Lisa Calderon and Senator Steve Padilla, and they are focused on addressing ongoing recovery challenges in wildfire-impacted communities across California.  The first bill, AB 1795, authored by Assemblymember Mike Gipson, would establish clear public health standards for smoke-damaged homes. After a wildfire, many homes may appear structurally sound but still contain hazardous smoke contamination. Without clear standards, homeowners often face delays, disputes, and uncertainty regarding remediation and insurance coverage. This bill creates clarity, protects public health, and helps streamline recovery.  The second bill, SB 876, authored by Senator Steve Padilla, strengthens disaster recovery protections and expands living expense coverage. Families displaced by wildfire are often out of their homes for extended periods. This measure helps ensure residents have adequate coverage for temporary housing and related costs, reducing financial hardship during a time of crisis.  The third bill, AB 1680, authored by Assemblymember Lisa Calderon, reforms and increases accountability within the FAIR Plan. As more Californians turn to the FAIR Plan due to insurance market instability, it is critical that the program operates with transparency, sustainability, and strong consumer protections. This bill aims to improve oversight and help stabilize the insurance market for wildfire-impacted communities.  Together, these measures are designed to speed recovery, reduce coverage gaps, increase accountability, and strengthen long-term community resilience.  Local government support is incredibly important in advancing policies that directly impact residents. I respectfully ask for your consideration and support.  I’m happy to provide fact sheets on each of these bills, along with a brief survey, and I would also welcome the opportunity to deliver a short 10-minute presentation at a future council meeting to provide a legislative update and answer any questions.  Thank you for your time, your leadership, and your continued commitment to protecting your community.  Dystanie Flores  Outreach Analyst  Community Relations and Outreach Branch  California Department of Insurance  300 South Spring Street, South Tower  Los Angeles, CA 90013  Dystanie.Flores@insurance.ca.gov Cell: (213) 220-7748  Twitter @CDINews Facebook @insurancecagov  [cid:image001.jpg@01DCAAFB.EAA86550]  CONFIDENTIALITY NOTICE: This communication may contain confidential and/or legally privileged information. Text: Subject: Follow-up email to CDI Legislative Update including PPT presentation  and useful links  Good afternoon,  It was a pleasure to meet you and present yesterday! Thanks so much, Chief Snyder, for inviting me and coordinating with the city. I appreciate each member of the City Council and the staff for your respectful hospitality.  Mayor Vallecillo, I appreciate you sharing your personal struggles with obtaining homeowner's insurance, which helped me clarify some points. I am sorry, the situation is grim right now.  Councilmember Loren, I appreciate your suggestion to send relevant materials to your office. I left some with Chief Snyder for you. I am happy to mail more. Just let me know the quantity you need.  As I mentioned, please fill out this survey with your feedback https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://www.surveymonkey.com/r/9PZWYX8__;!!NUwy8xNK!9-A8_YzJPRt0vT1b0LwpHNBHLD9CMyqYSDE7UFtzs-0v_2auZyIKFMcOlx6od9Z29GJqLiEkf14fUUPOfeGZR3AcU0w3kVYk$ .  Please see the attached fact sheet for the three bills shared: The Smoke Damage Recovery Act, The Disaster Recovery Reform Act, and The Make it Fair Act. I encourage you to support these legislative reforms by including a brief action alert in your next newsletter, urging constituents to back our wildfire insurance legislation and to engage directly with the California Department of Insurance. These bills enhance consumer protections, expand access to coverage, and promote safer, more resilient communities. Your support through a letter to the chair, sharing this information, and encouraging constituents to contact their legislators or attend a CDI presentation will significantly help advance these reforms. Please use this link to provide support: https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://bit.ly/CDILegAction__;!!NUwy8xNK!9-A8_YzJPRt0vT1b0LwpHNBHLD9CMyqYSDE7UFtzs-0v_2auZyIKFMcOlx6od9Z29GJqLiEkf14fUUPOfeGZR3AcU01IJCr6$ .  Sign up for our consumer alerts to stay updated on our efforts through legislation and regulations already passed https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://www.surveymonkey.com/r/CDIALERT__;!!NUwy8xNK!9-A8_YzJPRt0vT1b0LwpHNBHLD9CMyqYSDE7UFtzs-0v_2auZyIKFMcOlx6od9Z29GJqLiEkf14fUUPOfeGZR3AcU-EVYjtk$ .  Additionally, here are some links that may be useful for you and your constituents. Please feel free to contact me if I can help your office in any way.  General  If you have questions about your home, auto, business, life, or other insurance, contact the Department of Insurance at 800-927-4357 or visit us online at https://urldefense.com/v3/__http://www.insurance.ca.gov/__;!!NUwy8xNK!9-A8_YzJPRt0vT1b0LwpHNBHLD9CMyqYSDE7UFtzs-0v_2auZyIKFMcOlx6od9Z29GJqLiEkf14fUUPOfeGZR3AcU-3cWkWR$  Safer from Wildfires  Safer from Wildfires is an interagency partnership between Insurance Commissioner Ricardo Lara and the emergency response and readiness agencies in Governor Gavin Newsom's administration to protect lives, homes, and businesses by reducing wildfire risk. https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://www.insurance.ca.gov/01-consumers/200-wrr/Safer-from-Wildfires.cfm__;!!NUwy8xNK!9-A8_YzJPRt0vT1b0LwpHNBHLD9CMyqYSDE7UFtzs-0v_2auZyIKFMcOlx6od9Z29GJqLiEkf14fUUPOfeGZR3AcU4ML25wm$  Sustainable Insurance Strategy  For this presentation and more information about the sustainable insurance strategy:  https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://www.insurance.ca.gov/01-consumers/180-climate-change/SustainableInsuranceStrategy.cfm*:*:text=The*20primary*20goal*20of*20California's*20Sustainable*20Insurance*20Strategy*20is*20to,rules*20compared*20to*20other*20jurisdictions__;I34lJSUlJSUlJSUlJSUl!!NUwy8xNK!9-A8_YzJPRt0vT1b0LwpHNBHLD9CMyqYSDE7UFtzs-0v_2auZyIKFMcOlx6od9Z29GJqLiEkf14fUUPOfeGZR3AcU6ueDVpS$ .  Homeowners insurance  The Department of Insurance has several tools on our website to help you search the marketplace for residential insurance.  * Our Top Ten Tips for Finding Residential Insurance provides a summary of the information and options available: https://urldefense.com/v3/__http://www.insurance.ca.gov/01-consumers/105-type/5-residential/Top10Tips_FindingResidentialIns.cfm__;!!NUwy8xNK!9-A8_YzJPRt0vT1b0LwpHNBHLD9CMyqYSDE7UFtzs-0v_2auZyIKFMcOlx6od9Z29GJqLiEkf14fUUPOfeGZR3AcU1SaVPVc$  * Our Home Insurance Finder tool can help you identify insurance companies and licensed agents/brokers who have been identified to sell homeowners, renters, condominium, or mobile home insurance: https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://interactive.web.insurance.ca.gov/apex_extprd/f?p=400:50__;!!NUwy8xNK!9-A8_YzJPRt0vT1b0LwpHNBHLD9CMyqYSDE7UFtzs-0v_2auZyIKFMcOlx6od9Z29GJqLiEkf14fUUPOfeGZR3AcU7OZDsdo$  Additionally, here are links to our Senior Gateway website and the California Low Cost Auto Insurance (CLCA)Program. Text: Subject: FW: Public Comment: Request for Council Support – Wildfire Recovery Legislation  From: Flores, Dystanie  Sent: Wednesday, March 4, 2026 9:44 AM  To: 'ccpubliccomment@southpasadenaca.gov' Subject: FW: Public Comment: Request for Council Support – Wildfire Recovery Legislation  Mayor and Councilmembers.  My name is Dystanie Flores, and I serve as an Outreach Analyst with the California Department of Insurance. Thank you for the opportunity to speak with you today.  I’m respectfully request your support for a package of wildfire recovery and insurance reform bills currently under consideration at the State Capitol. These measures are authored by Assemblymember’s Mike Gipson, Lisa Calderon and Senator Steve Padilla, and they are focused on addressing ongoing recovery challenges in wildfire-impacted communities across California.  The first bill, AB 1795, authored by Assemblymember Mike Gipson, would establish clear public health standards for smoke-damaged homes. After a wildfire, many homes may appear structurally sound but still contain hazardous smoke contamination. Without clear standards, homeowners often face delays, disputes, and uncertainty regarding remediation and insurance coverage. This bill creates clarity, protects public health, and helps streamline recovery.  The second bill, SB 876, authored by Senator Steve Padilla, strengthens disaster recovery protections and expands living expense coverage. Families displaced by wildfire are often out of their homes for extended periods. This measure helps ensure residents have adequate coverage for temporary housing and related costs, reducing financial hardship during a time of crisis.  The third bill, AB 1680, authored by Assemblymember Lisa Calderon, reforms and increases accountability within the FAIR Plan. As more Californians turn to the FAIR Plan due to insurance market instability, it is critical that the program operates with transparency, sustainability, and strong consumer protections. This bill aims to improve oversight and help stabilize the insurance market for wildfire-impacted communities.  Together, these measures are designed to speed recovery, reduce coverage gaps, increase accountability, and strengthen long-term community resilience.  Local government support is incredibly important in advancing policies that directly impact residents. I respectfully ask for your consideration and support.  I’m happy to provide fact sheets on each of these bills, along with a brief survey, and I would also welcome the opportunity to deliver a short 10-minute presentation at a future council meeting to provide a legislative update and answer any questions.  Thank you for your time, your leadership, and your continued commitment to protecting your community.  Dystanie Flores  Outreach Analyst  Community Relations and Outreach Branch  California Department of Insurance  300 South Spring Street, South Tower  Los Angeles, CA 90013  Dystanie.Flores@insurance.ca.gov Cell: (213) 220-7748  Twitter @CDINews Facebook @insurancecagov  [cid:image001.jpg@01DCAAFB.EAA86550]  CONFIDENTIALITY NOTICE: This communication may contain confidential and/or legally privileged information. Please plan to arrive by 7:15 AM, or earlier if you have handouts.  * Location: Tukwet Golf Club, 36211 Champions Drive, Beaumont, CA.  * Introduction: You will be introduced by our Board President, Cesar Marrufo, at approximately 8:20 AM.  * Presentation Time: You will have between 25-35 minutes to present, followed by a Q&A session.  * PowerPoint: If you plan to use a PowerPoint presentation, please bring your own laptop and any necessary cables. Virtual Leg update with Supervisor Matt Dorsey's office
Met to discuss Legislative Update Presentation
Topic: CDI Overview, Fire Recovery, Homeowners, Insurance Issues, Safer from Wildfires, Sustainable Insurance Strategy

Text: 3 min public comment  Good afternoon, Mayor and Councilmembers.  My name is Dystanie Flores, and I serve as an Outreach Analyst with the California Department of Insurance. Thank you for the opportunity to speak with you today.  I’m calling to respectfully request your support for a package of wildfire recovery and insurance reform bills currently under consideration at the State Capitol. These measures are authored by Assemblymember’s Mike Gipson, Lisa Calderon and Senator Steve Padilla, and they are focused on addressing ongoing recovery challenges in wildfire-impacted communities across California.  The first bill, AB 1795, authored by Assemblymember Mike Gipson, would establish clear public health standards for smoke-damaged homes. After a wildfire, many homes may appear structurally sound but still contain hazardous smoke contamination. Without clear standards, homeowners often face delays, disputes, and uncertainty regarding remediation and insurance coverage. This bill creates clarity, protects public health, and helps streamline recovery.  The second bill, SB 876, authored by Senator Steve Padilla, strengthens disaster recovery protections and expands living expense coverage. Families displaced by wildfire are often out of their homes for extended periods. This measure helps ensure residents have adequate coverage for temporary housing and related costs, reducing financial hardship during a time of crisis.  The third bill, AB 1680, authored by Assemblymember Lisa Calderon, reforms and increases accountability within the FAIR Plan. As more Californians turn to the FAIR Plan due to insurance market instability, it is critical that the program operates with transparency, sustainability, and strong consumer protections. This bill aims to improve oversight and help stabilize the insurance market for wildfire-impacted communities.  Together, these measures are designed to speed recovery, reduce coverage gaps, increase accountability, and strengthen long-term community resilience.  Local government support is incredibly important in advancing policies that directly impact residents. I respectfully ask for your consideration and support.  I’m happy to provide fact sheets on each of these bills, along with a brief survey, and I would also welcome the opportunity to deliver a short 10-minute presentation at a future council meeting to provide a legislative update and answer any questions.  Thank you for your time, your leadership, and your continued commitment to protecting your community.

Text: Subject: RE: In-person meeting request by Association of California Cities  Orange County - 3/9 or 3/10  Good afternoon, Wendy,  My name is Durriya Syed, and I work as an Outreach Analyst for the CA Department of Insurance. I left you a voicemail earlier and am now following up via email regarding the meeting request below. I am available to present on March 9 on behalf of the Department of Insurance. Please confirm a suitable time and share your preferred focus areas for your group. Thank you!  Have a great weekend!  Best regards,  Durriya Syed  Outreach Analyst  Community Relations and Outreach Branch  California Department of Insurance  (916) 300-1225 (Mobile)  Event Information  Name Of Event: Association of California Cities Orange County Annual Sacramento Trip  TYPE Of Event: In-Person  Request Type: Meeting  If Other Explain:  Event Date & Time  Open to Change: N  Event Start Date: 09-Mar-2026  Event End Date: 10-Mar-2026  Requested Attendance Date for Commissioner:  Start Time:  End Time:  Preferred Time Commissioner is Asked To Participate:  Additional Comments: Meeting times are available between 1 pm and 4:30 pm on March 9th and 9 am to 11 am on March 10th.  Location of Event  Meeting Location: CDI Office - Sacramento  Other Location:  Venue: Citizen Hotel  Event Address: 926 J Street  Event City: Sacramento  Event State: CA  Event ZIP: 95814  Event Details  Brief Description: The Association of California Cities – Orange County (ACC-OC) represents the regional policy needs of Orange County cities and special districts. Collectively, our members provide services to up to 3.2 million people and work across county borders on a multitude of public policy issues at the local, state and federal level. I am writing on behalf of ACC-OC President Tanya Doby, Mayor of the City of Los Alamitos and the ACC-OC Board of Directors to extend an invitation to meet with our delegation. ACC-OC will be embarking on a two-day local government advocacy trip to Sacramento in support of our Members and region. This trip has become an annual opportunity for an Orange County delegation to focus on local government advocacy. Our delegation will include more than 25 elected officials and ACC-OC Affiliate Members – local community leaders from the non-profit, business, and higher education sectors. The ACC-OC delegation will spend two days advocating for smart policy solutions, listening to stakeholders on their positions, and collaborating with state legislators and agencies on important issues like: Housing & Homelessness, Transportation Solutions, Water Security, Infrastructure & Community Planning, and Budget and Financial Outlooks impacting local government. ? As the Insurance Commissioner, we would truly welcome an opportunity to meet with you for a 30-minute session on either Monday, March 17th between 1:00 p.m. and 4:30 p.m. or Tuesday, March 18th between 9 a.m. and 11:00 a.m. Meeting locations can be held in-office or at our meeting space at The Citizen Hotel (926 J St, Sacramento, CA 95814).  What is the goal of the Event?: Collaborating on insurance issues facing local regions including homeowners insurance and the impacts of wildfires and wildland areas.  What is the history/background of this event?:  Specific Issues/Topics: See Above  About the Audience: The Association of California Cities – Orange County (ACC-OC) represents the regional policy needs of Orange County cities and special districts. Text: Subject: Follow-up email to CDI Legislative Update including PPT presentation  and useful links  Good afternoon Christina,  It was a pleasure to catch up and present to you today! Please fill out this survey with your feedback https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://www.surveymonkey.com/r/9PZWYX8__;!!NUwy8xNK!_20hntY2GrDDDjdejjdNq9rsqD9URR3GnhlTOwh6n5gAnVj3txj6XAGS40Ki0iyVJzdv1x2lDsgjp9YmHfYAl2OWRkrte4ni$ .  Please find attached the PowerPoint presentation and fact sheet for the three bills shared: The Smoke Damage Recovery Act, The Disaster Recovery Reform Act, and The Make it Fair Act. I encourage you to support these legislative reforms by including a brief action alert in your next newsletter, urging constituents to back our wildfire insurance legislation and to engage directly with the California Department of Insurance. These bills enhance consumer protections, expand access to coverage, and promote safer, more resilient communities. Your support through a letter to the chair, sharing this information, and encouraging constituents to contact their legislators or attend a CDI presentation will significantly help advance these reforms. Please use this link to provide support: https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://bit.ly/CDILegAction__;!!NUwy8xNK!_20hntY2GrDDDjdejjdNq9rsqD9URR3GnhlTOwh6n5gAnVj3txj6XAGS40Ki0iyVJzdv1x2lDsgjp9YmHfYAl2OWRvQjN7yr$ .  Sign up for our consumer alerts to stay updated on our efforts through legislation and regulations already passed https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://www.surveymonkey.com/r/CDIALERT__;!!NUwy8xNK!_20hntY2GrDDDjdejjdNq9rsqD9URR3GnhlTOwh6n5gAnVj3txj6XAGS40Ki0iyVJzdv1x2lDsgjp9YmHfYAl2OWRpd_UmpD$ .  Additionally, here are some links that may be useful for you and your constituents. Please feel free to contact me if I can help your office in any way.  General  If you have questions about your home, auto, business, life, or other insurance, contact the Department of Insurance at 800-927-4357 or visit us online at https://urldefense.com/v3/__http://www.insurance.ca.gov/__;!!NUwy8xNK!_20hntY2GrDDDjdejjdNq9rsqD9URR3GnhlTOwh6n5gAnVj3txj6XAGS40Ki0iyVJzdv1x2lDsgjp9YmHfYAl2OWRpiWOGvM$  Safer from Wildfires  Safer from Wildfires is an interagency partnership between Insurance Commissioner Ricardo Lara and the emergency response and readiness agencies in Governor Gavin Newsom's administration to protect lives, homes, and businesses by reducing wildfire risk. https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://www.insurance.ca.gov/01-consumers/200-wrr/Safer-from-Wildfires.cfm__;!!NUwy8xNK!_20hntY2GrDDDjdejjdNq9rsqD9URR3GnhlTOwh6n5gAnVj3txj6XAGS40Ki0iyVJzdv1x2lDsgjp9YmHfYAl2OWRqP00Njq$  Sustainable Insurance Strategy  For this presentation and more information about the sustainable insurance strategy:  https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://www.insurance.ca.gov/01-consumers/180-climate-change/SustainableInsuranceStrategy.cfm*:*:text=The*20primary*20goal*20of*20California's*20Sustainable*20Insurance*20Strategy*20is*20to,rules*20compared*20to*20other*20jurisdictions__;I34lJSUlJSUlJSUlJSUl!!NUwy8xNK!_20hntY2GrDDDjdejjdNq9rsqD9URR3GnhlTOwh6n5gAnVj3txj6XAGS40Ki0iyVJzdv1x2lDsgjp9YmHfYAl2OWRpyaI4hj$ .  Homeowners insurance  The Department of Insurance has several tools on our website to help you search the marketplace for residential insurance.  * Our Top Ten Tips for Finding Residential Insurance provides a summary of the information and options available: https://urldefense.com/v3/__http://www.insurance.ca.gov/01-consumers/105-type/5-residential/Top10Tips_FindingResidentialIns.cfm__;!!NUwy8xNK!_20hntY2GrDDDjdejjdNq9rsqD9URR3GnhlTOwh6n5gAnVj3txj6XAGS40Ki0iyVJzdv1x2lDsgjp9YmHfYAl2OWRkKmJDDQ$  * Our Home Insurance Finder tool can help you identify insurance companies and licensed agents/brokers who have been identified to sell homeowners, renters, condominium, or mobile home insurance: https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://interactive.web.insurance.ca.gov/apex_extprd/f?p=400:50__;!!NUwy8xNK!_20hntY2GrDDDjdejjdNq9rsqD9URR3GnhlTOwh6n5gAnVj3txj6XAGS40Ki0iyVJzdv1x2lDsgjp9YmHfYAl2OWRpyO-Oeb$  Additionally, here are links to our Senior Gateway website and the California Low Cost Auto Insurance (CLCA)Program.  Thank you so much! Text: Subject: RE: Invitation to Speak about California Homeowners Insurance  Hello Mr. Castillo,  Please accept my sincere apologies for the delayed response, and thank you again for your patience.  I’m looking forward to presenting on Saturday, March 14, 2026, at 11:00 AM. I will come prepared with a presentation and bring sufficient flyers and informational materials for the approximately 100 residents expected to attend.  At this time, it will just be me attending. Please feel free to provide my name to the gate and security team for entry, and thank you for arranging reserved parking. I truly appreciate the coordination.  If there are any specific topics you would like emphasized during the presentation, please let me know so I can tailor the information to best serve your community.  Thank you again, and I look forward to meeting everyone.  Dystanie Flores  Outreach Analyst  Community Relations and Outreach Branch  California Department of Insurance  300 South Spring Street, South Tower  Los Angeles, CA 90013  Dystanie.Flores@insurance.ca.gov Cell: (213) 220-7748  Twitter @CDINews Facebook @insurancecagov  [cid:image001.jpg@01DCA682.21C7BE10]  From: William Castillo Sent: Tuesday, February 10, 2026 4:48 PM  To: Flores, Dystanie Subject: Re: Invitation to Speak about California Homeowners Insurance  Dear Dystanie,  It's nice to e-meet you as well.  Thank you for getting back to me. I appreciate the update.  As for our meeting on Saturday, March 14, 2026, at 11:00 AM, we would prefer a presentation. We would also appreciate any flyers or information that you might hand out as well. We would be expecting around 100 residents. As we get closer to our meeting I will contact you to find out if it's only you or anyone else that would be coming with you, as we are a gated guarded community. Our guards will have your name and anyone else coming with you. We will have reserved parking with your name or names.  Warm regards,  ~William Castillo  Vice president/Treasurer Oakridge Residents Association (ORA)  On Monday, February 9, 2026 at 01:31:29 PM PST, Flores, Dystanie > wrote:  Dear Mr. Castillo,  It is nice to e-meet you, and thank you for the invitation.  I am reaching out to confirm that I will be attending and providing a presentation on Saturday, March 14, at 11:00 AM regarding the homeowners insurance concerns at your mobile home park.  Please let me know if you would prefer for the presentation to be held in person or virtually, and I will be happy to accommodate whichever option works best for you.  I look forward to meeting you and speaking with the group.  Best regards,  Dystanie Flores  Outreach Analyst  Community Relations and Outreach Branch  California Department of Insurance  300 South Spring Street, South Tower  Los Angeles, CA 90013  Dystanie.Flores@insurance.ca.gov Cell: (213) 220-7748  Twitter @CDINews Facebook @insurancecagov  [cid:image001.jpg@01DCA682.21C7BE10]  From: William Castillo >  Sent: Friday, January 30, 2026 3:30 PM  To: Pina, Judith >  Cc: Wesley Maynez-ORA President >; Patricia Chitjian >; Kurt Doerr >  Subject: Invitation to Speak about California Homeowners Insurance  Dear Judith,  I hope you are feeling better. My name is William Castillo, and I serve as Vice President of the Oakridge Mobile Home Park Residents Association in Sylmar, CA  I am reaching out to warmly invite you or a representative from your office to speak at our upcoming Oakridge Residents Association meeting on Saturday, March 14, 2026, at 11:00 AM.  Oakridge is a private, gated community of 600 homes, and our residents have a strong interest in better understanding the insurance challenges currently affecting Californians and our Sylmar community in general. Your expertise as an Outreach Analyst with the California Department of Insurance would be invaluable to our homeowners, many of whom are navigating questions about coverage availability, policy changes, and consumer protections.  The meeting will be held at the Oakridge Clubhouse, and we anticipate a highly engaged group of residents.  Please let me know if you are available to join us. We would be honored to host you or another representative from your group and are happy to accommodate any logistical needs you may have.  Thank you for your time and consideration. I look forward to your response. Text: Subject: FW: Great meeting you at the Whittier Transportation Resource Fair  From: Flores, Dystanie  Sent: Wednesday, February 25, 2026 2:15 PM  To: 'Natalie Noel' Cc: Caloca, Brenda ; Felix Almeida, Estela Subject: RE: Great meeting you at the Whittier Transportation Resource Fair  Hi Natalie,  I hope you're doing well. I wanted to follow up regarding my previous introduction. I apologize for the confusion, but there has been a change on our end.  Brenda will no longer be covering the West Chester area. Instead, I’d like to introduce you to Estela, who will now be your primary contact moving forward.  Estela, Natalie is someone I believe you’ll enjoy connecting with. I’ll let the two of you coordinate directly to schedule a time to meet and explore ways to collaborate.  Again, my apologies for the mix-up, and thank you both for your flexibility.  Best regards,  Dystanie Flores  Outreach Analyst  Community Relations and Outreach Branch  California Department of Insurance  300 South Spring Street, South Tower  Los Angeles, CA 90013  Dystanie.Flores@insurance.ca.gov Cell: (213) 220-7748  Twitter @CDINews Facebook @insurancecagov  [cid:image001.jpg@01DCA661.30840830]  From: Flores, Dystanie  Sent: Tuesday, February 24, 2026 3:49 PM  To: 'Natalie Noel' >  Cc: Caloca, Brenda >  Subject: RE: Great meeting you at the Whittier Transportation Resource Fair  Hi Natalie,  Please accept my sincere apologies for the delay in responding to your email. I truly appreciate your patience, and I’m so glad we’re now able to connect.  Natalie, I’d love to introduce you to my colleague, Brenda. She covers the Westchester area and would be a wonderful resource for Westside Pacific Villages and your members when it comes to insurance education and presentations.  Brenda, Natalie works with Westside Pacific Villages, a fantastic nonprofit supporting older adults who are aging in place by providing transportation, home assistance, social programming, and more. They’re always looking to bring valuable educational resources to their community.  I’ll let you both take it from here to explore how you can collaborate. I’m confident this will be a great connection!  Thank you,  Dystanie  From: Natalie Noel >  Sent: Tuesday, February 10, 2026 2:43 PM  To: Flores, Dystanie >  Cc: LaTisha Anderson >  Subject: Re: Great meeting you at the Whittier Transportation Resource Fair  Hi Dystanie,  It's so nice to meet you! My name is Natalie Noel and I work with a non-profit called Westside Pacific Villages. We are based in Westchester (just north of LAX) and serve the westside of LA. We help older adults stay in their own homes, or age in place, by providing services like transportation, handyman help, technology support, friendly calls/visits, etc. by our wonderful network of volunteers. We also coordinate social programs and educational events as well.  So I am very glad to know of you and that you can be a resource to our amazing community when it comes to all things related to insurance. We are fairly booked up the next 3 months, but I have your contact information for when we would like a program presentation. How far in advance do you like to book those? I also see the flyer with upcoming webinars; we would be happy to share these with our members in the event they are interested in this info.  LaTisha - Thank you again for the introduction.  Hope you both have a great week!  Natalie  On Mon, Feb 9, 2026 at 1:36?PM Flores, Dystanie > wrote:  Hi LaTisha,  Thank you so much. I truly appreciate the referral.  Nice to meet you, Natalie and Spencer. I look forward to connecting with both of you and scheduling a presentation for your respective communities at Westside Pacific Village and Encino Terrace Senior Living. Subject: Great Connecting at the 211 LA County 45th Anniversary Event  Good afternoon,  It was a pleasure meeting you both at the 45th Anniversary celebration for 211 LA County. I truly enjoyed learning more about the impactful work you do to connect residents with vital community resources.  My name is Dystanie, and I am with the California Department of Insurance. I wanted to follow up to explore whether there may be an opportunity for collaboration between 211 LA County and our department. I am not certain if 211 currently shares information about our programs, but I would welcome the opportunity to connect and ensure you have details about several valuable resources we offer to the public.  One of these resources is our Senior Gateway program. The Senior Gateway is designed to assist older Californians with insurance-related questions and concerns. It provides direct access to specially trained staff who help seniors navigate Medicare supplement insurance, long-term care insurance, life insurance policies, annuities, and other related matters. The goal is to ensure seniors and their families have trusted guidance and protection when making important insurance decisions.  Additionally, we administer the California Low Cost Automobile Insurance Program (CLCA). This program helps income-eligible good drivers obtain affordable, state-minimum liability auto insurance coverage. It is intended to make auto insurance accessible to those who might otherwise struggle to afford it, while still ensuring compliance with California’s financial responsibility laws.  Beyond these programs, the California Department of Insurance regulates the state’s insurance marketplace and assists consumers with insurance-related issues, including filing complaints, resolving disputes with insurance companies, and answering coverage questions. We serve as a resource for Californians who need guidance or support with any insurance matter.  Since 211 operates at the county level and we are a state agency, I would love to connect to see how we might work together to ensure residents are aware of these services. If this information is not already included in your resource database, I would be happy to provide materials or coordinate further.  Thank you again for your time today. I look forward to staying in touch and exploring potential ways to collaborate in serving our communities.  Warm regards,  Dystanie Flores  Outreach Analyst  Community Relations and Outreach Branch  California Department of Insurance  300 South Spring Street, South Tower  Los Angeles, CA 90013  Dystanie.Flores@insurance.ca.gov  Cell: (213) 220-7748  Twitter @CDINews Facebook @insurancecagov  CONFIDENTIALITY NOTICE: This communication may contain confidential and/or legally privileged information. Subject: Request for Council Support – Wildfire Recovery Legislation  Dear Mayor and Councilmembers,  Good afternoon,  My name is Dystanie Flores, Outreach Analyst with the California Department of Insurance. I am reaching out to request your support for a package of wildfire recovery and insurance reform bills currently under consideration at the State Capitol.  I have attached fact sheets on the legislation along with a brief survey. I would also welcome the opportunity to provide a short 10-minute presentation at an upcoming City Council meeting to share a legislative update and answer questions.  The bills focus on:  • Establishing clear public health standards for smoke-damaged homes (AB 1795)  • Strengthening disaster recovery protections and expanding living expense coverage (SB 876)  • Reforming and increasing accountability within the FAIR Plan (AB 1680)  Together, these measures aim to speed recovery, reduce coverage gaps, increase accountability, and stabilize California’s insurance market for wildfire-impacted communities.  Local government support is important in advancing policies that protect residents and strengthen community resilience. I respectfully ask for your consideration and would be happy to coordinate with your staff regarding a presentation opportunity. Text: Subject: Follow-up email to CDI Legislative Update including PPT presentation  and useful links  Good afternoon Bryan,  It was a great pleasure to meet and present to you yesterday! Sometimes this world feels so small! Please fill out this survey with your feedback https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://www.surveymonkey.com/r/9PZWYX8__;!!NUwy8xNK!_ewFM29imIS07VyW_0xcFYJH-SZh9C4sAFP_bmM73VPMJOuVzs3_Ns1El2FU5CpRJmaDh7bP7l3EPD48YJzbBLiztfRDEXls$ .  Please find attached the PowerPoint presentation and fact sheet for the three bills shared: The Smoke Damage Recovery Act, The Disaster Recovery Reform Act, and The Make it Fair Act. I encourage you to support these legislative reforms by including a brief action alert in your next newsletter, urging constituents to back our wildfire insurance legislation and to engage directly with the California Department of Insurance. These bills enhance consumer protections, expand access to coverage, and promote safer, more resilient communities. Your support through a letter to the chair, sharing this information, and encouraging constituents to contact their legislators or attend a CDI presentation will significantly help advance these reforms. Please use this link to provide support: https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://bit.ly/CDILegAction__;!!NUwy8xNK!_ewFM29imIS07VyW_0xcFYJH-SZh9C4sAFP_bmM73VPMJOuVzs3_Ns1El2FU5CpRJmaDh7bP7l3EPD48YJzbBLiztcMp3Pc7$ .  Sign up for our consumer alerts to stay updated on our efforts through legislation and regulations already passed https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://www.surveymonkey.com/r/CDIALERT__;!!NUwy8xNK!_ewFM29imIS07VyW_0xcFYJH-SZh9C4sAFP_bmM73VPMJOuVzs3_Ns1El2FU5CpRJmaDh7bP7l3EPD48YJzbBLiztSWVsZFc$ .  Additionally, here are some links that may be useful for you and your constituents. Please feel free to contact me if I can help your office in any way.  General  If you have questions about your home, auto, business, life, or other insurance, contact the Department of Insurance at 800-927-4357 or visit us online at https://urldefense.com/v3/__http://www.insurance.ca.gov/__;!!NUwy8xNK!_ewFM29imIS07VyW_0xcFYJH-SZh9C4sAFP_bmM73VPMJOuVzs3_Ns1El2FU5CpRJmaDh7bP7l3EPD48YJzbBLiztdSOaMbd$  Safer from Wildfires  Safer from Wildfires is an interagency partnership between Insurance Commissioner Ricardo Lara and the emergency response and readiness agencies in Governor Gavin Newsom's administration to protect lives, homes, and businesses by reducing wildfire risk. https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://www.insurance.ca.gov/01-consumers/200-wrr/Safer-from-Wildfires.cfm__;!!NUwy8xNK!_ewFM29imIS07VyW_0xcFYJH-SZh9C4sAFP_bmM73VPMJOuVzs3_Ns1El2FU5CpRJmaDh7bP7l3EPD48YJzbBLiztcpuEfng$  Sustainable Insurance Strategy  For this presentation and more information about the sustainable insurance strategy:  https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://www.insurance.ca.gov/01-consumers/180-climate-change/SustainableInsuranceStrategy.cfm*:*:text=The*20primary*20goal*20of*20California's*20Sustainable*20Insurance*20Strategy*20is*20to,rules*20compared*20to*20other*20jurisdictions__;I34lJSUlJSUlJSUlJSUl!!NUwy8xNK!_ewFM29imIS07VyW_0xcFYJH-SZh9C4sAFP_bmM73VPMJOuVzs3_Ns1El2FU5CpRJmaDh7bP7l3EPD48YJzbBLiztb4U6Du_$ .  Homeowners insurance  The Department of Insurance has several tools on our website to help you search the marketplace for residential insurance.  * Our Top Ten Tips for Finding Residential Insurance provides a summary of the information and options available: https://urldefense.com/v3/__http://www.insurance.ca.gov/01-consumers/105-type/5-residential/Top10Tips_FindingResidentialIns.cfm__;!!NUwy8xNK!_ewFM29imIS07VyW_0xcFYJH-SZh9C4sAFP_bmM73VPMJOuVzs3_Ns1El2FU5CpRJmaDh7bP7l3EPD48YJzbBLiztZl5rCUJ$  * Our Home Insurance Finder tool can help you identify insurance companies and licensed agents/brokers who have been identified to sell homeowners, renters, condominium, or mobile home insurance: https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://interactive.web.insurance.ca.gov/apex_extprd/f?p=400:50__;!!NUwy8xNK!_ewFM29imIS07VyW_0xcFYJH-SZh9C4sAFP_bmM73VPMJOuVzs3_Ns1El2FU5CpRJmaDh7bP7l3EPD48YJzbBLiztSX_gJ3v$  Additionally, here are the links to our Senior Gateway website and the California Low Cost Auto Insurance (CLCA)Program.  Thank you so much! Text: Subject: Request to Provide Insurance Legislative Update at City Council  Meeting  Hello,  I trust this email finds you well. My name is Judith Piña, and I serve as the Outreach Analyst for the California Department of Insurance under Commissioner Ricardo Lara, I cover Riverside and San Bernardino Counties.  I am reaching out to request the opportunity to provide a brief legislative and insurance market update at an upcoming City Council meeting. My goal would be to share information on recent state insurance legislation, consumer protection efforts, and resources available to residents - including programs such as the California Low Cost Auto Insurance Program (CLCA).  The presentation would be approximately 10-15 minutes.  Thank you for your time, I look forward to hearing from you.  Warm regards,  Judith Pina  Outreach Analyst  Community Relations and Outreach Branch  California Department of Insurance  300 South Spring Street, South Tower  Los Angeles, CA 90013  (213) 507-9973  Judith.Pina@insurance.ca.gov Twitter @CDINews Facebook @insurancecagov  CONFIDENTIALITY NOTICE: This communication may contain confidential and/or legally privileged information. Brenda – Yes, the 10am at the Social Services Center where you went last year is in Spanish – they sometimes have one or two people that speak English. Is that date still good for you? Thanks,  BRYAN JONSON  Fire Marshal  [CWS-Email-Logo]  Fire Department  2040 Lake Washington Boulevard  West Sacramento, CA 95691  Telephone: (916) 617-4608  bryanj@cityofwestsacramento.org [facebook 24px] [twitter 24px] [youtube 24px] [cid:image005.gif@01DCA58C.42EC2910] From: Syed, Durriya >  Sent: Friday, February 20, 2026 10:24 AM  To: Jonson, Bryan >  Cc: Howell, Brenna >  Subject: RE: Requesting a brief meeting with your office to provide a legislative update  CAUTION: This email originated from outside of the organization. Best regards,  Durriya Syed  Outreach Analyst  Community Relations and Outreach Branch  California Department of Insurance  (916) 300-1225 (Mobile)  From: Jonson, Bryan >  Sent: Thursday, February 19, 2026 3:05 PM  To: Syed, Durriya >  Cc: Howell, Brenna >  Subject: FW: Requesting a brief meeting with your office to provide a legislative update  Good afternoon, Durriya, and thank you for reaching out.
