Thank you,  Jennifer Cusmir, CMC, MPA  City of West Sacramento | City Clerk  1110 West Capitol Ave | West Sacramento, CA 95691  Office: (916) 617-4528  jenniferc@cityofwestsacramento.org From: Syed, Durriya >  Sent: Tuesday, February 10, 2026 2:38 PM  Subject: Requesting a brief meeting with your office to provide a legislative update  CAUTION: This email originated from outside of the organization. Do not click links or open attachments unless you recognize the sender and know the content is safe.  Hello,  I hope you are doing well. My name is Durriya Syed. I am the Outreach Analyst for the California Department of Insurance serving your area.  I'm reaching out to request a brief meeting with your office to provide a legislative update on the Make It FAIR Act and the Disaster Recovery Reform Act. These measures have important implications for disaster resilience, insurance protections, and recovery planning. We would value the opportunity to share background updates and answer any questions your office may have.  Please let me know your availability over the next few weeks. I'm happy to accommodate your schedule, whether virtually or in person. Looking forward to hearing back from you. Subject: RE: Requesting a brief meeting with your office to provide a  legislative update.  Hi Eric,  It was a pleasure speaking with you. I reached out to all the council members; my email was forwarded, and the Fire Chief, Rustice, also contacted me. I plan to virtually meet with him on February 24 at 1:00 PM. If you're available then, I can send a Teams invite to either the Mayor or you. Please let me know if that works for you. If not, we can find a more convenient time.  I've attached the PowerPoint presentation I plan to share with you and the participants. It will address homeowners' insurance concerns, including a broader framework for recovery and preparedness.  I hope this information is helpful. Feel free to contact me with any questions or concerns. Thank you, and have a great weekend!  Best regards,  Durriya Syed  Outreach Analyst  Community Relations and Outreach Branch  California Department of Insurance  (916) 300-1225 (Mobile)  From: Syed, Durriya  Sent: Thursday, February 12, 2026 2:54 PM  Subject: Requesting a brief meeting with your office to provide a legislative update.  Hello,  My name is Durriya Syed. I am the Outreach Analyst for the California Department of Insurance serving your area. Please find the PowerPoint presentation attached. Below are some links that could be helpful for you and your constituents. Feel free to reach out if I can assist your office in any way.  General  If you have questions about your home, auto, business, life, or other insurance, contact the Department of Insurance at 800-927-4357 or visit us online at http://www.insurance.ca.gov/  Safer from Wildfires  Safer from Wildfires is an interagency partnership between Insurance Commissioner Ricardo Lara and the emergency response and readiness agencies in Governor Gavin Newsom's administration to protect lives, homes, and businesses by reducing wildfire risk. https://www.insurance.ca.gov/01-consumers/200-wrr/Safer-from-Wildfires.cfm  Sustainable Insurance Strategy  For this presentation and more information about the sustainable insurance strategy:  https://www.insurance.ca.gov/01-consumers/180-climate-change/SustainableInsuranceStrategy.cfm#:~:text=The%20primary%20goal%20of%20California's%20Sustainable%20Insurance%20Strategy%20is%20to,rules%20compared%20to%20other%20jurisdictions.  Homeowners insurance  The Department of Insurance has several tools on our website to help you search the marketplace for residential insurance.  * Our Top Ten Tips for Finding Residential Insurance provides a summary of the information and options available: http://www.insurance.ca.gov/01-consumers/105-type/5-residential/Top10Tips_FindingResidentialIns.cfm  * Our Home Insurance Finder tool can help you identify insurance companies and licensed agents/brokers who have been identified to sell homeowners, renters, condominium, or mobile home insurance: https://interactive.web.insurance.ca.gov/apex_extprd/f?p=400:50  How to become a Firewise Community  * https://www.readyforwildfire.org/prepare-for-wildfire/firewise-communities/  Thanks! Text: Good morning Dystanie,  I hope you're doing well. It was a pleasure connecting with you at the City's Community Preparedness Day in September, thank you again for joining us!  I wanted to follow up regarding the possibility of you presenting at an upcoming City Council meeting. Could you share a bit more about the topics you typically cover in your presentations? Subject: Reintroduction and follow-Up to Partnership Opportunities  Hello Kamilah,  I hope this email finds you well. It was a pleasure meeting you. I wanted to take a moment to reintroduce myself, my name is Judith Piña, and I serve as an Outreach Analyst with the California Department of Insurance. In my role, I work closely with community partners to educate the community about the Department’s services, consumer protections, and key insurance related initiatives. Many people are not aware that department exists or the resources and assistance we provide, which is why community partnerships like yours are so important. We also offer presentations that can be tailored to meet the needs of the specific audience you serve.  As mentioned during our conversation, I am sharing information about the California Low Cost Auto Insurance (CLCA) Program. This state-sponsored program provides affordable liability auto insurance to income-eligible drivers who meet certain requirements, helping individuals stay legally insured while protecting themselves and others on the road. I have attached a digital copy for your reference, along with links to senior resources and a link to our one pagers below. Our one pagers are available in multiple languages. I would be happy to drop off printed materials at your office as well.  I truly appreciate the opportunity to connect and look forward to exploring ways we can work together to support our community. Please feel free to reach out with any questions or partnership ideas. Hi Dystanie,  It's so nice to meet you! My name is Natalie Noel and I work with a non-profit called Westside Pacific Villages. We are based in Westchester (just north of LAX) and serve the westside of LA. We help older adults stay in their own homes, or age in place, by providing services like transportation, handyman help, technology support, friendly calls/visits, etc. by our wonderful network of volunteers. We also coordinate social programs and educational events as well.  So I am very glad to know of you and that you can be a resource to our amazing community when it comes to all things related to insurance. We are fairly booked up the next 3 months, but I have your contact information for when we would like a program presentation. How far in advance do you like to book those? I also see the flyer with upcoming webinars; we would be happy to share these with our members in the event they are interested in this info.  LaTisha - Thank you again for the introduction.  Hope you both have a great week!  Natalie

Good morning, Chief Snyder,  I hope you're doing well! It was a pleasure to meet you virtually. Below is the information you requested. Please let me know if you need anything else. Happy holidays to you and your loved ones!  1. The Safer From Wildfires regulation, which has been approved, allows consumers to obtain discounts on their premiums if they complete wildfire risk mitigation efforts on their property. They also qualify for a discount if they are in a Firewise USA Site in Good Standing or a Fire Risk Reduction Community. Additionally, this regulation requires insurance companies to provide you with your wildfire risk score and the factors that went into creating that score. More information may be found on our Safer From Wildfires page: https://www.insurance.ca.gov/01-consumers/200-wrr/Safer-from-Wildfires.cfm  2. Sustainable Insurance Strategy: It will benefit consumers in the following ways:  1. Increased insurance availability because insurance companies will write more homes and businesses in wildfire-distressed areas.  2. Return FAIR Plan policyholders to traditional insurance, with priority given to hardened homes and businesses following the Safer from Wildfires regulation.  3. Allowing the utilization of forward-looking catastrophe models prioritizing wildfire safety, mitigation, and fuel reduction. Insurance companies will increase their writing because they better anticipate future losses.  4. More information may be found on the Department’s Sustainable Insurance Strategy Page: Sustainable Insurance Strategy 3. Use our Home Insurance Finders Tool (https://interactive.web.insurance.ca.gov/apex_extprd/f?p=400:50) for Insurance Companies and Brokers/Agents in your area.  4. Top Ten Tips for Finding Residential Insurance (https://www.insurance.ca.gov/01-consumers/105-type/5-residential/Top10Tips_FindingResidentialIns.cfm)  5. Fire Risk Reduction Community (Link for FRRC) - https://calfire-umb05.azurewebsites.net/media/qgdhfsll/2025-frrcl-brochure.pdf  6. United Policyholders (UP) is a non-profit 501(c) (3) organization whose mission is to be a trustworthy and valuable information resource and an effective voice for consumers of all types of insurance in all 50 states.  1. United Policyholders Website: https://uphelp.org/about/  2. For questions for United Policyholders: info@uphelp.org Please sign up for our Consumer Alert so you can stay updated.  Thank you! California Department of Insurance: Brenda Caloca  Board of Forestry and Fire Protection In response to the increased frequency and intensity of wildfires in California over the last decade, the Legislature passed AB 3074 (2020)External Link and SB 504 (2024)External Link, making changes to Public Resource Code 4291External Link. These changes require the Board of Forestry and Fire Protection (Board) to establish an ember-resistant defensible zone the first 5 feet around a structure, referred to as Zone 0. Governor Newsom’s Executive Order N-18-25 requires that the Board complete rulemaking for Zone 0 no later than December 31, 2025.

Subject: Congratulations from the California Department of Insurance  Hello,  I hope this message finds you in good spirits. My name is Durriya Syed, and I serve as an outreach analyst with the California Department of Insurance. I wish to take a moment to acknowledge and commend your exceptional efforts in supporting California drivers through the California Low Cost Auto (CLCA) program.  Your dedication to making auto insurance accessible and affordable is particularly commendable. Last year, you demonstrated remarkable commitment by issuing numerous CLCA policies, thereby creating a positive impact within the communities you serve. Your work genuinely exemplifies professionalism and a heartfelt commitment to assisting more Californians in obtaining necessary coverage.  As a token of appreciation, we would be honored to present you with a formal certificate. Kindly inform us of your preferred method of receipt:  * Formal Presentation: We can arrange a brief virtual or in-person event to recognize your achievements.  * Mailed Certificate: Alternatively, we can mail the certificate directly to your office.  Please respond to this email with your preferred option. Should you have any inquiries or wish to discuss further, do not hesitate to contact me directly at 916-300-1225 or via email at Durriya.Syed@insurance.ca.gov.  Thank you very much for your continued dedication to the CLCA program and your invaluable contribution to enhancing auto insurance accessibility throughout California. Congratulations once again on this well-deserved recognition! 2026 Small Business Pop-Up Fair: Recovering Together  By LA County DEO Office of Small Business  Follow  Loma Alta Park Gymnasium  Jan 8 from 12pm to 4pm PST  Overview  A recovery-focused community event and marketplace marking one year since the January 2025 wildfires.  One year after the January 2025 wildfires, as the community continues to come together to recover, rebuild, and support Altadena’s small businesses. The Los Angeles County Department of Economic Opportunity (DEO) invites small business owners, entrepreneurs, workers, and residents to attend Recovering Together, a free, recovery-focused community pop-up fair and marketplace in Altadena and surrounding areas.  Hosted in partnership with Kathryn Barger, the LA County Department of Parks and Recreation, and the Altadena Chamber of Commerce, this event is designed to meet small businesses where they are offering practical help, real connections, and a chance to shop local.  What to Expect  * 1:1 Technical Assistance & Recovery Resources Meet directly with County and community partners to get help with commercial permitting, new capital and grant programs, pro bono legal aid, hiring incentives for youth and permanent staff, and more.  * On-Site Service Partners Confirmed partners include DEO and LA County agencies—Public Works, Regional Planning, Treasurer-Tax Collector, and Parks—alongside trusted community organizations such as Los Angeles County Economic Development Corporation, Pasadena City College Small Business Development Center, Pasadena City College Women’s Business Center, Bet Tzedek, and ICON CDC.  * Shop Local LA County Marketplace Presented in partnership with the Altadena Chamber of Commerce, the marketplace features local small businesses—giving residents a meaningful way to support neighbors and keep dollars in the community.  Whether you’re a small business owner seeking recovery support, an entrepreneur exploring next steps, or a resident who wants to shop local and show up for Altadena, Recovering Together is for you.  Admission is free. Register today and be part of Altadena’s recovery.  Show less  Category: Business, Startups  Good to know  Highlights  * 4 hours  * In person  Loma Alta Park Gymnasium  3330 North Lincoln Avenue  Altadena, CA 91001
Offices: Supervisor Kathryn Barger (Los Angeles County, CA)
Organizations: Los Angeles County, California; Department of Economic Opportunity County of Los Angeles (office of Small Business )

Text: Subject: FW: Top 100 Producers for the California Low Cost Auto Insurance  Program Recognition  Hello:  My name is Judith Pina with the California Department of Insurance under the leadership of Commissioner Ricardo Lara. I serve as the Department's liaison for your service area.  It is truly a pleasure to congratulate your organization on being recognized as one of our Top 100 Producers for the California Low Cost Auto Insurance (CLCA) Program. This is an important achievement, and we would be honored to present you with a certificate recognizing your work.  We are currently coordinating with your local Senate and Assembly offices to offer an in-person certificate presentation. Before moving forward, I wanted to check in with you to see what you would prefer.  Please let us know:  * If you would be interested in an in-person presentation, and your availability, or  * If you would prefer for the certificate to be mailed to you instead.  Thank you again for your continued partnership and for helping make affordable auto insurance accessible to Californians. I look forward to hearing from you. Thank you for the information - appreciate it!  Sherry Wolcott  Sherry.wolcott@cbrealty.com 530-391-3884  ________________________________  From: Syed, Durriya >  Sent: Thursday, December 11, 2025 4:23 PM  To: Wolcott, Sherry L >  Subject: RE: Social Media Resources  Good afternoon Sherry,  Thank you for reaching out to me. I'm sorry for this late response. I just wanted to let you know that I left you a voicemail about my delayed response. Attached, please find the Social Media post. As I shared, here are some essential links to help you better serve your community: our two programs (SFW & SIS), the Home Insurance Finders Tool, the Top Ten Tips for Finding Residential Insurance, and preliminary lists of wildfire-distressed area counties and zip codes.  1. The Safer From Wildfires regulation, which has been approved, allows consumers to obtain discounts on their premiums if they complete wildfire risk mitigation efforts on their property.
